title: Expenditure and Tax Multipliers
description: Practice what you've learned about calculating, applying, and interpreting the expenditure multiplier and tax multiplier in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
The nation of Johnsrudia has a marginal propensity to consume (MPC) of $0.9$.

**If there is an autonomous increase in new home construction of $\$10$ billion, what will happen to real gross domestic product (GDP)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Real GDP will increase by more than $\$10$ billion
Real GDP will increase by less than $\$9$ billion
Real GDP will increase by exactly $\$10$ billion
Real GDP will increase by exactly $\$9$ billion
Real GDP will not change

assessment_items.question_content:
Fred got a year-end bonus in two different years. The table below describes how much of that bonus he spent in each year.

Bonus | Amount spent
-|-
$\$1000$ | $\$750$
$\$2000$ | $\$1500$

**If Fred's habits represent the typical household in this country, what is the marginal propensity to save in this country?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

*Assume Gerbilia's marginal propensity to save (MPS) is $0.3$.* 

**By how much will household consumption increase as a result of an increase in disposable incomes of $\$2000$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the changes described below would be the most likely reason for a $\$40$ billion increase in Bloominonionland's real GDP?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The government spent $\$15$ billion to upgrade Bloominonionland's highway network
The government increased taxes by $\$10$ billion
Bloominonionland's households decrease exports to its neighbor Wakanda by $\$45$ billion
Real interest rates in Bloominonionland were increased from $4\%$ to $6\%$
Investment in Bloominonionland's mining sector increased by $\$40$ billion

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the purpose of the expenditure multiplier?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: quantify the size of the change in aggregate demand (AD) that occurs as a result of a change in a component of AD
quantify the size of the change in aggregate demand (AD) that occurs as a result of a change in taxes
quantify the change in the size of the money supply that occurs as a result of a change in the reserve ratio
quantify the change in the interest rate that will occur as a result of an increase deficit spending
quantify the change in aggregate demand (AD) that will occur as a result of a change in government spending, but not other components of AD

assessment_items.question_content:
Investment spending in Gerbilia decreased by $\$6$ billion. 

**What measure can be used to determine the final impact of the change in investment spending on aggregate demand (AD)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The expenditure multiplier
The tax multiplier
No calculation is needed because AD will decrease by $\$6$ billion
The money multiplier
The balanced budget multiplier

assessment_items.question_content:
The marginal propensity to consume (MPC) in Hamsterville is $0.75$.

**What will be the size of the change in aggregate demand that will occur as a result of a $\$1$ million dollar increase in the government spending component of AD?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $\$0.75$ million
$\$4$ million
$\$0.25$ million
$\$3$ million
$\$1.75$ million

assessment_items.question_content:
Gerbilia decreased lump-sum taxes by $\$6$ billion. 

**What measure can be used to determine the final impact on aggregate demand that occurs as a result of the change in lump-sum taxes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The expenditure multiplier
The tax multiplier
No calculation is needed because AD will decrease by $\$6$ billion
The money multiplier
The balanced budget multiplier

assessment_items.question_content:
Households in Maggietopia save $40\%$ of every additional dollar in income that they receive. 

**What will happen to aggregate demand Maggietopia if there is a $\$2$ billion increase in lump-sum taxes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: If citizens save $40\%$ of every additional dollar earned, the marginal propensity to save (MPS) is $0.4$.

The formula for the tax multiplier is:

$-\dfrac{MPC}{MPS}$

The marginal propensity to consume (MPC) is:

$\begin{align}1 - MPS &= 1 - 0.4\\\\
& = 0.6\end{align}$

To find the effect of a change in taxes on GDP, multiply the tax multiplier by the change in taxes:

$\text{change in GDP from a change in taxes} = \text{change in taxes} \times \text{tax multiplier}$

GDP will increase by $\$3$ billion:
$-\$2 \text{  billion} \times -1.5 = \$3 \text{ billion}$

choices.content: Real GDP will decrease by $\$3$ billion
Real GDP will decrease by $\$5$ billion
Real GDP will decrease by $\$2$ billion
Real GDP will increase by $\$3$ billion
Real GDP will increase by $\$5$ billion

assessment_items.question_content:
Households in Iron Island save $10\%$ of every additional dollar in income that they receive. 

**What will happen to aggregate demand Iron Island if there is a $\$4$ billion increase in lump-sum taxes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Real GDP will decrease by $\$36$ billion
Real GDP will decrease by $\$40$ billion
Real GDP will decrease by $\$18$ billion
Real GDP will increase by $\$36$ billion
Real GDP will increase by $\$4$ billion

assessment_items.question_content:
*Hamsterville, where the marginal propensity to save is equal to $0.5$, is currently experiencing a negative output gap of 
$\$100$ million.* 

**How much would Hamsterville's government have to change taxes to restore the full employment level of aggregate output?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Taxes should be reduced by $\$100$ million
Taxes should be reduced by $\$50$ million
Taxes should be reduced by $\$10$ million
Taxes should be increased by $\$50$ million
Taxes should be increased by $\$100$ million

assessment_items.question_content:
*Hamsterville, where the marginal propensity to save is equal to $0.25$, is currently experiencing a negative output gap of 
$\$60$ million.* 

**How much would Hamsterville's government have to change taxes to restore the full employment level of aggregate output?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Taxes should be reduced by $\$20$ million
Taxes should be reduced by $\$10$ million
Taxes should be reduced by $\$5$ million
Taxes should be increased by $\$15$ million
Taxes should be increased by $\$60$ million

assessment_items.question_content:
*Assume that the typical household in Jabberville will spend $80\%$ of any additional increase in income.*

**How much will aggregate demand (AD) change as a result of a $\$4 $ billion increase in net export spending in Jabberville?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Real GDP increases by $\$20$ billion
Real GDP increases by $\$5$ billion
Real GDP increases by $\$4$ billion
Real GDP decreases by $\$4$ billion
Real GDP decreases by $\$16$ billion

assessment_items.question_content:
**If the marginal propensity to consume (MPC) decreases from $0.8$ to $0.6$, how will the marginal propensity to save (MPS) and the spending multiplier change?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The MPS increases; spending multiplier decreases
The MPS decreases; spending multiplier decreases
The MPS increases; no change in the spending multiplier
The MPS increases; the spending multiplier increases
No change to the MPS, the spending multiplier increases

assessment_items.question_content:
**What will happen to the size of the expenditure multiplier and the size of the tax multiplier when the marginal propensity to save (MPS) increases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Both decrease
Both increase
The size of the tax multiplier increases, the size of the spending multiplier decreases
The size of the tax multiplier doesn't change, the size of the spending multiplier increases
The size of the tax multiplier increases, the size of the spending multiplier doesn't change
